h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: I was letting the copied words settle the meaning
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I like the snap of that, but "forcing" may be too much if the room only pauses.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was converging on 'turned back' as the settled caption verb, needing only the room's reaction to confirm it.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Try the print test. Write one caption sentence with "turned back" and no extra explanation. Then read the copied line under CHAIR AT THE TABLE, pause, and read the same line under PROTESTER FROM THE FLOOR. If the caption makes the chair's office answer from the floor, it can go to print. If it only says the line appeared twice, "turned back" is doing work the evidence has not made visible.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: After L2's false closure ('I get it… I want to check whether that sounds too strong'), the tutor abandons the annotation/underlining approach and invents a falsifiability test — a representation shift from marking to drafting and reading aloud — that forces the learner to expose the verb to failure rather than continue seeking confirmatory evidence.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot L4, so no uptake of the learner's revised framing ('forcing' vs. 'making the table answer') is available to score above 3.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I was letting the copied words settle the meaning
